title: Calculating months, days, years
excerpt: I am looking for a formula that takes an employees hire date subtracted from todays date and tells me how long they have been employed in the form of xdays, xmonths, xyears , all I can figure out is how to calculate the …

posts.author: Johnsa9 (Ashley Morrison)
posts.cookedHtml: I am looking for a formula that takes an employees hire date subtracted from todays date and tells me how long they have been employed in the form of xdays, xmonths, xyears , all I can figure out is how to calculate the total number of days.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Johnsa9 - while I could not get this working in a native monday Formula column, I was able to make it work with General Caster. Here is the formula:

DATEDIF(0,DAYS(TODAY(),{item’s Start Date}),“y”)&" years " &
DATEDIF(0,DAYS(TODAY(),{item’s Start Date}),“ym”)&" months “&
DATEDIF(0,DAYS(TODAY(),{item’s Start Date}),“md”)&” days"

@JCorrell - DATEDIF isn’t supported in monday, any thoughts on how to do this directly in a monday formula column?

Hope this helps!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mark.anley

This will get pretty close:

INT(YEARFRAC(FORMAT_DATE({Date}), FORMAT_DATE(TODAY()))) & " Years " &
INT(MOD(YEARFRAC(FORMAT_DATE({Date}), FORMAT_DATE(TODAY())), 1) * 12) & " Months " &
INT(MOD(YEARFRAC(FORMAT_DATE({Date}), FORMAT_DATE(TODAY())) * 12, 1) / 12 * 365) & " Days"

Jim - The Monday Man
Column Magic - the magical columns toolbox app

posts.author: Johnsa9 (Ashley Morrison)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you! This worked exactly as I needed it to. I downloaded the Column magic app for another board and it worked for a second but now none of the columns are populating the formulas! I am not sure what to do.

posts.author: Johnsa9 (Ashley Morrison)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you! I like that general caster however I could only use it so many times in a month and I exceeded that.
